so ive been using this on test account on silver for 1 min and it works well with some modifications. First off  i use a 200 ema for trend and dont go against trend. If i complete a trade (sell for instance)i wait for it to break back above and close above the line. I wait for second candle to form and close without touching the line If the next candle closes back below the line then ill take another trade. I also dont use set stops. If it closes above where i would set stop ill close it but its to protect me against  stop hunting.  Id also be careful of major news events as well

You can not trade intelligently with only using the 9 EMA. Tell viewers they need to use it in conjunction with other tools such as a volume indicator, MACD, Fibonacci levels,  draw trend lines or support and resistance. Using EMA's alone is not enough to make a trading decision, because there are so many factors to consider before taking a position. Price can close above the 9 EMA and fake you out in a blink of an eye, and start going to opposite direction. Your video should add this so the amateur traders can be aware and protect against losing their money.

3:45  The "9/20 strategy" may work, but looking at that sell example, it was a losing move to wait for the EMAs to cross over.  If you waited for the actual crossover, then you got in at that green candle, getting stopped out.  The buy example was okay, though.  I wonder if you use a high enough risk/reward, riding the strong trends, you can stay profitable.

Even looking at the charts in these examples shows you a highly dangerous strategy. You are hand picking examples when it works but if you look at your rules earlier in the SAME chart it did not work. A risk to reward of 1 to 1 is going to make you broke. If you are new to trading please use a demo account and be aware of the amount of pure nonsense online. Totally irresponsible.
